what_it_does: Registers a single read-only MCP tool named corpus that multiplexes six corpus operations through an op selector (query/list/find/read/neighbours/determine), wrapping existing Suite methods and delegating to runtime modules for rerank, dragnet determine, and neighbourhood search.
observations: Single tool replaces prior flat list_corpus/find_corpus/read_corpus_record tools and newly exposes query and determine.
Decorated with @mcp.tool(annotations=ToolAnnotations(posture='safe')) to mark it read-only and client-safe.
The op parameter is a Literal of six string values; unknown ops return a teaching error listing valid choices.
A nested _shape helper truncates results to limit and projects to high-signal fields unless detail='detailed'.
query calls suite.query_corpus and optionally runs a jina-v3 rerank pass via runtime.corpus_rerank, default off.
In query with detail='detailed', each hit's record content is fetched inline by address.
determine delegates to runtime.recall_determine.determine, explicitly passing the warm suite and store.
read normalizes code:// paths, supports extraction:// records, falls back from source id to newest record, and returns teaching errors instead of silent nulls.
All operations are reads; there are no write/resolve/approve/dispatch paths.
standards: Inline warm-path fix comment explains passing the resident suite into recall_determine to avoid cold-constructing a new Suite on every call.
Honest-empty/teaching-error pattern: missing records produce explanatory errors with next-step guidance rather than bare nulls.
extraction:// read path lets the same tool read dragnet extraction-layer records, not just rank them.
Optional rerank stage is fail-loud when a hit's digest text cannot be resolved.
conventions: Heavy docstring and inline design-principle comments.
Literal type used for the op enum.
Default detail='concise' and limit=50 to keep responses token-efficient.
Runtime modules are imported lazily inside conditional branches.
Reuses existing Suite methods rather than adding a new engine.
concerns: The corpus function is large and deeply nested, mixing routing, validation, enrichment, and error handling.
Lazy imports inside branches obscure the file's runtime dependencies.
limit is reused both for list/find truncation and, in determine, as max_claims with a hard clamp at 120, which may surprise callers.
Parameter defaults and validation logic may drift from the underlying Suite method contracts.
notes: This is the consolidated read-only corpus MCP tool that routes six query operations through the warm Suite, with optional rerank, inline detailed answers, and dragnet extraction access.
questions: What embeddable spaces are actually available, and where are they enumerated?
What are the exact return shapes of suite.query_corpus and recall_determine.determine?
How does runtime.corpus_rerank surface missing-digest failures to callers?
Does top_n have any effect when rerank is False?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it consolidates corpus tools, exposes query/determine/neighbours, stays read-only, and reuses existing Suite methods.
apparent_themes: Honest failure surfaces with teaching errors.
Warm reuse of resident Suite state.
Grounded, source-traced retrieval via determine and neighbours.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make the corpus the single, unified read-only retrieval interface for the MCP face, reducing tool count while surfacing semantic query, dragnet grounded synthesis, and relational neighbourhood search, all while being explicit about coverage limits and failures.
novelty: The op-selector single-tool pattern replaces multiple flat tools, and the same tool integrates extraction:// reading plus a determine grounded-theme synthesis path.
contribution: Provides a consolidated, read-only corpus access point that multiplexes list/find/read/query/neighbours/determine, with optional rerank, concise/detailed projection, honest error messages, and warm reuse of the resident Suite.
summary_for_embedding: mcp_face/tools/corpus.py registers a single read-only MCP corpus tool with an op selector that routes query, list, find, read, neighbours, and determine operations through the warm Suite, supporting optional rerank, concise/detailed projection, and dragnet extraction reading.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate corpus read access into one token-efficient, honest, client-safe MCP tool that exposes semantic retrieval, grounded synthesis, and neighbourhood search without adding a new engine.

symbols.does: Registers the read-only corpus MCP tool with the given mcp instance and suite.
symbols.description: Top-level registration function that accepts an mcp object and a warm Suite instance, defines the nested corpus tool function and its _shape helper, and returns the tool so it can be attached to the MCP face.

symbols.does: Multiplexes six read-only corpus operations via an op selector and returns shaped results.
symbols.description: The MCP-decorated corpus tool accepts an op Literal and many optional filters. It validates the operation, routes to Suite methods or runtime modules for query/list/find/read/neighbours/determine, applies optional rerank, detailed content enrichment, code:// address normalization, extraction:// reading, and honest teaching errors. It is annotated with ToolAnnotations(posture='safe') to declare read-only, client-safe behavior.

symbols.does: Truncates and projects a list of corpus rows based on detail and limit.
symbols.description: Inner helper used by the list and find operations. It caps the row list at limit and, when detail is not 'detailed', returns only the high-signal fields source_address, projection, seq, and address to keep responses token-efficient.
